description_long: This object appears to be a long, slender, and irregularly shaped piece of sea glass or mineral, with a predominantly greenish-blue hue and a rough, crystalline texture. The surface is uneven, with jagged edges and a mix of translucent and opaque areas, suggesting natural weathering or erosion by water. The coloration transitions from a deeper green at one end to a lighter, almost white shade at the other, with some speckled inclusions visible throughout. The object is elongated and tapers to a point at one end, while the opposite end is broader and more blunt. Its overall appearance is reminiscent of a shard of mineral or glass that might be found washed ashore or submerged in a shallow coastal area. The texture and coloration evoke the look of sea-worn glass or a mineral fragment shaped by the ocean.
description: A long, jagged, greenish-blue shard with a rough, crystalline texture and a gradient from deep green to pale white. Its surface is uneven and weathered, resembling sea glass or a mineral fragment.
description_view_0: A compact, foreshortened view showing the blunt, broader end and highlighting the rough, uneven surface.
description_view_1: A side profile revealing the full length and slender, tapering shape, with visible color gradation and jagged edges.
description_view_2: A top-down view emphasizing the elongated, irregular outline and the transition from green to white along its length.
description_view_3: Another side profile from the opposite angle, showing the pointed tip and the rough, crystalline surface texture.
